topics.topic: Parliamentary Procedure Principles and Robert's Rules

questions.question: Which edition of Robert's Rules of Order, Newly Revised, is the official reference for the FBLA Parliamentary Procedure event?
questions.Help: Consult the 'Knowledge Areas' section of the guidelines.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Main Motion?
options: To introduce a new item of business to the assembly
To request information from the chair
questions.correctAnswer: To introduce a new item of business to the assembly
questions.Help: Main motions are the vehicle for bringing business before the assembly.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered a Privileged Motion?
questions.Help: Privileged motions deal with the rights of members and the meeting process itself.

questions.question: An incidental motion generally:
options: Relates to the pending business or the rules of the meeting
Is used to bring a previous question back before the assembly
Has the lowest precedence in parliamentary law
questions.correctAnswer: Relates to the pending business or the rules of the meeting
questions.Help: Incidental motions arise out of the current situation of the meeting.

questions.question: What is the correct order of business for a standard chapter meeting?
options: New Business, Old Business, Reports, Call to Order
Call to Order, Reports, Old Business, New Business
Call to Order, New Business, Old Business, Adjournment
Reports, Call to Order, New Business, Adjournment
questions.correctAnswer: Call to Order, Reports, Old Business, New Business
questions.Help: Meetings follow a logical sequence starting with the Call to Order.

questions.question: What is the effect of an 'Amend' motion?
options: To postpone the motion to a future time
To modify the wording of a pending motion
questions.correctAnswer: To modify the wording of a pending motion
questions.Help: Amend is a subsidiary motion used to change the pending main motion.

questions.question: What is the 'previous question' used for?
options: To ask for a vote on the minutes of the previous meeting
To immediately close debate and force a vote
To table a motion until the next meeting
To request that a motion from a past meeting be reconsidered
questions.correctAnswer: To immediately close debate and force a vote
questions.Help: The previous question is a subsidiary motion to terminate debate.

questions.question: A 'Point of Order' is used when:
options: A member wants to speak out of turn
A member notices a breach of the rules
A member wishes to move to adjourn
A member wants to change their vote
questions.correctAnswer: A member notices a breach of the rules
questions.Help: It is an incidental motion used to enforce rules.

questions.question: What happens if a motion dies for lack of a second?
options: The assembly votes on it anyway
The chair must provide a second
The motion is not considered by the assembly
The secretary records the motion in the minutes as 'Failed'
questions.correctAnswer: The motion is not considered by the assembly
questions.Help: Most motions require a second to be formally brought before the assembly.

questions.question: Which of these is a motion that brings a question again before the assembly?
questions.Help: These motions provide a way to revisit a previously decided item.

questions.question: What is the deadline for FBLA national membership dues for the current school year?
questions.Help: Check the Eligibility Requirements section.

questions.question: How many teams can each state submit for the Parliamentary Procedure event?
questions.Help: State entry limits are explicitly stated in the guidelines.

questions.question: What is the penalty for being late to the assigned presentation time?
questions.Help: Penalty points section provides the deduction amount.

questions.question: How many notecards are allowed per competitor during the role play?
questions.Help: Check the Role Play Details section.

questions.question: What determines which teams advance to the role play round?
options: The individual scores of the team members
questions.Help: Check the Scoring section.

questions.question: How long is the preparation time for the role play scenario?
questions.Help: Check the Timing Structure section.

questions.question: Which item are competitors NOT allowed to bring into the role play room?
options: A printed copy of the role play
questions.Help: Scripts are strictly prohibited.

questions.question: What happens if a student has placed in the top 10 of an event at a previous NLC?
options: They can never compete in any event again
They must compete in a different event category
They are ineligible to compete in that same event in future NLCs
They are allowed to compete, but their score is halved
questions.correctAnswer: They are ineligible to compete in that same event in future NLCs
questions.Help: Review the 'Repeat Competitors' policy.

questions.question: Where must competitors stay to be eligible for NLC competition?
options: Any hotel within the host city
A hotel designated by the school district
With a family member in the host city
questions.Help: Official Hotel Requirement section.

questions.question: What is the duration of the objective test?
questions.Help: Check the 'Test Duration' section.
